question: After running `docker compose up -d` from the developer Docker Compose quickstart, you open `http://localhost:8070` to use Web Modeler but the page does not load. What is the most likely reason?

options.text: Web Modeler is part of the full configuration only; the default `docker compose up -d` starts the lightweight configuration, which does not include Web Modeler.
Web Modeler must be enabled in `docker-compose.yaml` by setting the `WEB_MODELER_ENABLED=true` environment variable on the Orchestration Cluster service.
Web Modeler is served at `http://localhost:8088/modeler` in the lightweight configuration; port `8070` is reserved for the full configuration's Console.
Web Modeler rejects the default `demo`/`demo` credentials; you must first sign into Keycloak at `http://localhost:18080/auth/` with `admin`/`admin`.

a.text: The Access components section lists Web Modeler under 'Management and modeling components (full configuration only)' at `http://localhost:8070`. The lightweight stack started by `docker compose up -d` does not include Web Modeler — you need to start the full configuration with `docker compose -f docker-compose-full.yaml up -d`.

b.text: The docs do not document any `WEB_MODELER_ENABLED` toggle on the lightweight compose file. Web Modeler ships only with the full configuration, where it is wired up as its own service rather than enabled via an environment flag.

c.text: Port `8088` is used by the Orchestration Cluster (Operate, Tasklist, Identity, REST API), not Web Modeler. Web Modeler is on `http://localhost:8070`, and Console runs on `http://localhost:8087` in the full configuration.

d.text: Even with correct credentials, Web Modeler is simply not running under the lightweight configuration, so no login flow would appear. The `admin`/`admin` credentials belong to Keycloak, which is also only started by the full configuration.

explanation: The Docker Compose quickstart distinguishes a lightweight configuration (default `docker compose up -d`, with the Orchestration Cluster on `:8088` and Elasticsearch) from a full configuration (`docker compose -f docker-compose-full.yaml up -d`) that additionally starts Console on `:8087`, Optimize on `:8083`, Management Identity on `:8084`, Web Modeler on `:8070`, plus Keycloak and PostgreSQL. To use Web Modeler locally, start the full configuration.
